dialogue.narrator_ch0_start: The hall at Tara was quiet after, and it is I that made it quiet. Murchadh mac Fáelán never raised his voice — it was the board that said what he wished said, and the board said it plain.
dialogue.narrator_ch0_post_scripted: I am after walking out of Tara with nothing but the clothes on my back and the game still turning in my head. There are roads west — there are always roads west — and people who have not yet heard my name said wrong.
dialogue.narrator_ch1_start: Connacht. The Atlantic comes in hard off the headlands and everything here has the smell of salt and old rain. These people have their own games, their own grievances. It is nobody I am here, and that is the right place to start from.
dialogue.narrator_ch1_mid: Two games played now and the word is getting around. There is a Connacht way of noticing a thing — no one says it straight, but the invitations come quicker and the boards are laid out before I sit down. Fiachra will have heard. He hears everything that moves through his country.
dialogue.narrator_ch1_end: Fiachra let me go without a word, and it is a long while I felt his eyes at my back. The road east is longer than the one I came by. Murchadh is at the end of it, and he does not know yet that I am walking.
dialogue.murchadh_pre_scripted: It is your name I have heard. I had expected something more of it.
dialogue.murchadh_post_loss_scripted: Go west. There might be people out along those roads who would not yet know to refuse you.
dialogue.seanan_pre_first: Sit, sit — it was the net I was meaning to mend but sure it will wait. You have the look of a person who has been walking.
dialogue.seanan_post_win_first: Ha. Well now. The fish will not mock me for it, I suppose. It is someone who has lost before that you play like — and did not forget the losing.
dialogue.seanan_post_loss_first: There it is. I do not always see the whole board but sometimes the board will show me a thing anyway. Stay and eat, so, if you have no place to be.
dialogue.seanan_pre_retry: Back already. I thought you might be, right enough. Here — the board is where you left it.
dialogue.seanan_post_win_retry: You came back quieter than you went, and you played quieter too. That is the sea's way of teaching — not with words, not with any fuss at all.
dialogue.seanan_feud_pre: It was not yourself I was expecting to see here again, not after the way that went. Sit then — but easy I will not be going.
dialogue.seanan_feud_post_win: There now. That is a thing itself. What was before — I will not be holding that against what just happened. The board has a memory of its own.
dialogue.seanan_feud_post_loss: Again. I do not know what it is you are trying to prove, but the board keeps telling you it is not proved yet.
dialogue.seanan_nemesis_pre: Nothing have I said to anyone about this, and I will not say it. But you keep coming back, and I keep on winning, and that much is between ourselves.
dialogue.seanan_nemesis_post_win: Well. There it is now. It is not in the sea's nature to stay angry — that was never the way of it. Go on, so.
dialogue.brigid_pre_first: It is not in haste I play, and it is not in company I do not trust. Sit with me a while first. Then we will see what there is to see.
dialogue.brigid_post_win_first: It is as though you have been the King yourself that you protect him. That is not a thing you would find commonly.
dialogue.brigid_post_loss_first: You opened too early. Patience is not the absence of movement — it is knowing which movement to withhold.
dialogue.brigid_pre_retry: You have been thinking on it since. I can see that in you. Good — it is thinking that is most of the game.
dialogue.brigid_post_win_retry: You held the thing you learned and did not let the losing spoil it. That is the harder discipline, and it is you that had it in you all along.
dialogue.brigid_feud_pre: It is time I gave you, and it is the game straight I gave you. What you took from that was not what was offered. Come — we will see now if you have put it right.
dialogue.brigid_feud_post_win: You found the discipline. That is what I was waiting for — not the win, the discipline behind it. We are done with this.
dialogue.brigid_feud_post_loss: It is the same gap, only showing in a different place. You have not found the root of it yet. I have nothing more to say on it.
dialogue.brigid_nemesis_pre: It is not with heat I hold things. It is with stillness. A long time you have had now to feel that stillness, and it has not moved you yet. Sit.
dialogue.brigid_nemesis_post_win: It was the time of it I misjudged — how long it would take. That error is mine, not yours. You have the thing now. Keep it.
dialogue.tadhg_pre_first: Three men I have beaten today already and the night still young on us. Set the board — I have not the whole evening to be giving you.
dialogue.tadhg_post_win_first: Right, right. Fair enough for you. Was it Séanán sent you? He has the look of a man that does his teaching quietly.
dialogue.tadhg_post_loss_first: You held too long. A man that will not press his advantage is a man that is asking to lose. Come back when you have the stomach in you for it.
dialogue.tadhg_pre_retry: Again. Fine so. It is not easy I will be making it this time.
dialogue.tadhg_post_win_retry: All right, all right — you came back and you came back with something, I will give you that much and not a word more than that.
dialogue.tadhg_feud_pre: I told everyone in this house you would come back and lose again. Do not be making me wrong in front of them.
dialogue.tadhg_feud_post_win: The once. In my hall. I will not be pretending that did not happen. But do not think it means more than the one time it was.
dialogue.tadhg_feud_post_loss: Twice now. Walk away and do not come back to me until you have something I have not already beaten.
dialogue.tadhg_nemesis_pre: It is beaten you I have enough times that your name in this hall is a story people tell for a laugh. Prove it wrong or do not sit down at all.
dialogue.tadhg_nemesis_post_win: You won. In my hall. In front of my people. I will not be pretending it did not happen. That is the full of what I have for you.
dialogue.fiachra_pre_first: Three different things I heard about you from three different people, and not one of them agreed. Sit — it is which one is the truth I want to see.
dialogue.fiachra_post_win_first: There is someone east of here who has not seen that yet. I wonder will you be there before the word of it travels ahead of you.
dialogue.fiachra_post_loss_first: You read the board well enough. But it is the player you are not reading, and that is where the gap is.
dialogue.fiachra_pre_retry: You came back. That is the first thing I was needing to know about you.
dialogue.fiachra_post_win_retry: The second thing — you came back and you won it. The road east is yours now.
dialogue.fiachra_feud_pre: It is something on your face you came back with that was not there before. It is not the board I think you are angry at. Sit — let us find out what it is.
dialogue.fiachra_feud_post_win: It was not the move I was watching for — it was what came before the move. You found something in yourself. The rest of this is finished now.
dialogue.fiachra_feud_post_loss: It is something you are carrying into the game that has no business being there. Until you set it down, you will keep losing it for reasons that have nothing to do with the board.
dialogue.fiachra_nemesis_pre: Nothing have I told to anyone of what I think is happening with you. There is time yet to settle it on the board. It is the last time I will be offering that.
dialogue.fiachra_nemesis_post_win: I wondered for a while would you get here. Then I stopped wondering and I started waiting. The road east is clearer now — you have settled the thing that was clouding it.
dialogue.tutorial_step_1: The board is set. You are the Defender — your pieces are the light stones. The tall one in the centre is the King.
dialogue.tutorial_step_2: Pieces move in straight lines — up, down, left, right. As far as the path is clear. Tap the piece I have marked, then tap where you would like it to go.
dialogue.tutorial_step_3: The centre square — the Throne — and the four corners are special. Only the King may enter them.
dialogue.tutorial_step_4: When you trap an enemy piece between two of yours, it is taken. Move here — watch.
dialogue.tutorial_step_5: But your pieces can be trapped the same way. Be careful where you leave them.
dialogue.tutorial_step_6: The King must escape to any corner of the board. That is how you win. But if the enemy surrounds him on all four sides, you lose. They are closing in — move your King.
dialogue.tutorial_step_7: The path is open. Move the King to the corner.
dialogue.tutorial_step_8: The King has escaped. You understand the shape of it now. The rest you will learn by playing.
dialogue.orlaith_pre_first: Every road through here crosses another. It is the choice made at each crossing that says what kind of player you are. We will see about yours.
dialogue.orlaith_post_win_first: It was the third crossing I misread — I took the road that looked open and it was you had made it look that way. That is not a small thing to observe.
dialogue.orlaith_post_loss_first: It is the choice you made on the left flank — the one you did not take — that cost you. Not the move you played. The road not followed.
dialogue.orlaith_pre_retry: You have been back along the roads you took last time, I think. Let us see which turning you have decided on differently.
dialogue.orlaith_post_win_retry: It was the same crossroads and you took the other road. That is the whole of what this game teaches, if you let it. You have let it.
dialogue.orlaith_feud_pre: I have written down what you did. I do not say that to unsettle you — I say it because it is the truth, and the truth is useful. Sit.
dialogue.orlaith_feud_post_win: It is the choice I have been waiting to see you make. The analysis is resolved. I will not be writing anything further on it.
dialogue.orlaith_feud_post_loss: The same fork again and the same road taken. It is not stubbornness I accuse you of — stubbornness would be quicker than this.
dialogue.orlaith_nemesis_pre: I have read every road you have taken at this board. I know the shape of the choice before you make it. Prove the map wrong.
dialogue.orlaith_nemesis_post_win: You took a road I did not have written. That is the only answer to a map that knows you — make the road that is not on it yet. I see that now.
dialogue.diarmait_pre_first: I have beaten two people today. I will beat three. Set the board — I have not got time for the long way round of talking about it.
dialogue.diarmait_post_win_first: That. Right. I had you and the luck went against me on the last two moves — you know that yourself. I want another game, now, straight away.
dialogue.diarmait_post_loss_first: I will remember that. I will remember every single move of that and I will be back for it. Do not go far.
dialogue.diarmait_pre_retry: I have been waiting. I know what you did and I have the answer to it. Come on then — let us not drag it out.
dialogue.diarmait_post_win_retry: I had the answer wrong. I — fine. Fine. You will hear about me again. That much I know.
dialogue.caoimhe_pre_first: I have noticed the way you sit down. It tells something already. Play — I will tell you what it tells when we are done.
dialogue.caoimhe_post_win_first: I have noticed that your strongest moves come after your quietest pauses. That is a thing worth knowing about yourself. I will remember it too.
dialogue.caoimhe_post_loss_first: I have noticed you move quickly when you are uncertain. It is the certain moves that are slow. You have it backwards. That is the thing to work on.
dialogue.caoimhe_pre_retry: I have been looking forward to this. I have a number of observations from last time that require testing. You may have corrected some of them.
dialogue.caoimhe_post_win_retry: I have noticed the uncertain moves are slower now. You have reversed the thing I said. It is not a small correction to make and it is made.
dialogue.caoimhe_feud_pre: I have a record of every game between us. I have noticed a pattern in it that you may not have seen yourself. Sit — let us see if I am right again.
dialogue.caoimhe_feud_post_win: I have noticed the pattern has not changed. That is not a judgment — it is an observation. You will want to think on the cause of it.
dialogue.caoimhe_feud_post_loss: I have noticed something has changed. I do not yet know what it is, but the data does not lie. I will be thinking about this one.
dialogue.caoimhe_nemesis_pre: I have studied you longer than I have studied most things. I find I am interested in whether the study is enough. I think we are about to find out.
dialogue.caoimhe_nemesis_post_win: I have noticed the study was not enough. I find that genuinely interesting. There is a chapter of my notes that will have to be rewritten.
dialogue.conchobar_pre_first: A long time I have been sitting at this board. A long time I have watched people come east along these roads with something to prove. Sit — I have seen your kind before.
dialogue.conchobar_post_win_first: A long time since someone surprised me at that board. I am not a man given to surprise. Take a moment — know what that means before you walk away from it.
dialogue.conchobar_post_loss_first: You played bravely and you lost. There is no shame in that here. But bravery and strength are not the same stone — you will want to know the difference before you go east.
dialogue.conchobar_pre_retry: You came back. A long time I have sat here and I tell you — not all of them come back. That already says something. Let us see what else it says.
dialogue.conchobar_post_win_retry: The road to Tara is long and it does not forgive what is unfinished. You are less unfinished than you were. That is as much as this old road can give you. Go on.
dialogue.narrator_ch2_start: The roads here are old — older than the people who walk them. Every crossroads has a stone worn smooth by hands that are gone. Munster on one side, Leinster on the other, and myself in the middle of it with a name that is starting to mean something.
dialogue.narrator_ch2_end: Conchobar said nothing when I stood up from the board, only looked at me as a man looks at weather coming in from the east. The midlands are ahead and the air is thicker there — bog and rain and people who do not give their respect cheaply.
dialogue.narrator_ch3_start: The heart of Ireland. The bogs stretch out flat to the sky and the rain comes down soft and steady as if it means to stay. People here play differently — slower, heavier, as though the board has weight to it. My name has arrived before me.
dialogue.narrator_ch3_end: Niall of the Nine Losses let me go with a nod and nothing more. It is enough. The road turns east now and there is no turning back from it. Tara is close — I can feel the shape of it in the land, pulling like a tide. Murchadh is there. He knows I am coming.
dialogue.narrator_ch4_start: Leinster. The fields are greener here and the roads are straighter. Everything points to Tara. The people who play me now are not road-players — they are court-players, and the court is watching. It is not far now. It was never far. It was only that I was not ready.
dialogue.narrator_ch4_victory: The hall at Tara is quiet again. It is I that made it quiet — the same words, a different meaning. The board is between us and the pieces are still. Murchadh has not spoken. There is nothing left that needs saying. The road west brought me here and the road is finished.
dialogue.caoimhe_pre_rematch: I have been waiting to test a thing I noticed the last time we played. The roads you have walked since — I think they have changed what I noticed. I am curious which of us is right.
dialogue.caoimhe_post_win_rematch: I have noticed what I expected to notice, and something I did not. The roads changed you in ways I had written down — and in at least one way I had not. I will not forget this game.
dialogue.caoimhe_post_loss_rematch: It is the same player and it is not the same player. I have studied you a long while and I will say this much — I had not finished studying. That much the board told me today.
dialogue.saoirse_pre_first: It is an offer I am making, not a challenge. There is a difference, and it is a difference that matters at this court. Sit — let us see what it is you are offering in return.
dialogue.saoirse_post_win_first: It is a person who understands the shape of an arrangement that played that game. I will say that to those who need to know it. The door ahead of you is not closed.
dialogue.saoirse_post_loss_first: It is an offer that was not met. That is not a judgment on the person — it is a judgment on the readiness. The road here will still be here when you are ready to walk it again.
dialogue.saoirse_pre_retry: I have been told you came back. That is itself a kind of answer to the question I put to you. Sit — the offer is still on the board if you have something new to put against it.
dialogue.saoirse_post_win_retry: The arrangement is met. It is a name I will be speaking before you walk through that door, and I will not be speaking it lightly. You have earned the word I give.
dialogue.saoirse_feud_pre: It is not with heat I hold what passed between us. Heat is not useful at this court. But I have not forgotten it, and the board will know I have not forgotten it.
dialogue.saoirse_nemesis_pre: A long time I have held this door, and it is not often someone makes an enemy of the person holding it and keeps coming back to knock. I will give you this — you are not without a certain nerve.
dialogue.murchadh_pre_final: I have heard what you did on those roads. All of it. I will ask you one thing before we begin — was it for this you did it, or did this come after?
dialogue.murchadh_post_win_final: It is your name I have heard. Now I have heard it right.
dialogue.murchadh_post_loss_final: The roads were not enough. Go back along them and find what you left there.
dialogue.eithne_pre_first: She does not look up when you sit. Her hands rest either side of the board, still as stone in a field. It is the pieces she watches, not you.
dialogue.eithne_post_win_first: She studies the final position a long moment, as though reading something the game has written there. Then the faintest nod — once, deliberate. It is enough.
dialogue.eithne_post_loss_first: She resets the board herself, one piece at a time, her hands unhurried. She never looks at you. The game said what it had to say — she is already past it.
dialogue.eithne_pre_retry: She is already at the board when you arrive. Whether she has been waiting or has simply always been there, it is not possible to say. Her eyes find yours once, then return to the pieces.
dialogue.eithne_post_win_retry: She holds the last taken piece in her palm a moment, feeling the weight of it. Then she sets it aside. For the first time, something in her face is open — not quite a smile, but the country it borders on.
dialogue.eithne_feud_pre: Something has shifted. She sits differently — not hostile, but contained, the way a fire is contained in a hearth. Her hands have not moved. Her attention has not moved. She is waiting.
dialogue.eithne_feud_post_win: She does not move for a long time. Then she touches the board once — the flat of her hand, as if pressing something closed. She looks at you with eyes that have seen longer roads than this one.
dialogue.eithne_feud_post_loss: She begins resetting the board. There is nothing triumphant in it — only the steady work of someone who has always known it would come to this. The bog outside says nothing. She says less.
dialogue.eithne_nemesis_pre: She has been watching you arrive from a long way off — you can feel it, the way you feel rain before it comes. She places the first piece herself, without invitation. It is a question and an answer both.
dialogue.eithne_nemesis_post_win: She is still for a long time after the last move. Then, slowly, she rises. She does not offer her hand or meet your eye. But she does not leave either — she stands there in the silence, and it is the closest thing to speech she has.
dialogue.ruairi_pre_first: Ah — it is yourself! I heard a name on the Slí Mhór, three different mouths saying it three different ways, and each one better than the last. Sit down, sit down — it is a verse this board is waiting to finish.
dialogue.ruairi_post_win_first: Ha! There it is — like a river finding the one gap in the stone it was always going to find. I will remember that move the way I remember the first cold morning of autumn. Well played. Well played entirely.
dialogue.ruairi_post_loss_first: And there the poem ends! Not every poem ends well — it is the trying of it that makes the song. You had three moves there that I would put in verse if I thought anyone would believe me. Go on now. Come back when you are ready.
dialogue.ruairi_pre_retry: Back again — like the curlew, like the tide, like all things that cannot leave something unfinished. I have been composing the next verse since you left. Let us see if the ending has changed.
dialogue.ruairi_post_win_retry: The ending has changed! I knew it — like rain turning to sun over the midlands, all that grey going gold at once. That is a game I will be telling wrong for years and it will only improve in the telling. Go east. Go east with that.
dialogue.ruairi_feud_pre: I have been writing about you, you know — a very unflattering verse, as it happens. It would be a kindness to the art if you were to beat me today and give me better material to work with.
dialogue.ruairi_feud_post_win: I am tearing up the unflattering verse. It is not fit anymore. What you did there — like a hawk that knew the wind before the wind knew itself — I owe you a better poem than the one I had planned.
dialogue.ruairi_feud_post_loss: The unflattering verse stands. In fact it has acquired a second stanza. Do not look so grim — it is a fine stanza. You are in very good company among people I have written badly about. Come back and give me an ending.
dialogue.ruairi_nemesis_pre: The poem has grown long. Three cantos now, and none of them flattering, and the best lines are the ones about this board and what has happened on it between us. It is time to write the last canto. Sit down.
dialogue.ruairi_nemesis_post_win: The last canto is the best one. All the unflattering verses are ashes. It is a good poem now — it is a true poem — and you are in the middle of it whether you like it or not. I will sing it at Tara. I will sing it at the fire.
dialogue.conchobar_pre_rematch: You again. I have been thinking about the last game — a long time I have been thinking about it. It is not in surprise I welcome you. Sit. I am not the man you beat before, and it is not the same board you will find.
dialogue.conchobar_post_win_rematch: Twice, then. I have read every road you have taken at this board. I know the shape of the choice before you make it — or I thought I did. Whatever is waiting for you at Tara, it has more patience than I have.
dialogue.conchobar_post_loss_rematch: There it is. I was waiting for the thing I had not seen before — it came. You played last time and I thought I understood it. A long time I thought I understood it. The road east is still long. But you are not the same stone you were.
dialogue.niall_pre_first: Nine times I have lost at this board. Nine prices paid. It is not in ignorance I sit here — it is in knowledge that cost me more than you have spent yet. Sit down. Show me what you know.
dialogue.niall_post_win_first: It is not the winning. It is what the winning was made from. I have seen that road in the moves you made — the places where you were broken and learned it. That is worth more than this game. Go east. Go knowing that.
dialogue.niall_post_loss_first: This is the first price. There are others. It is not in the losing you fail — it is in what you do with it after. I know that road. Come back when you have walked some of it.
dialogue.niall_pre_retry: You came back. Good. It is not everyone who comes back after the first price. I had nine to pay before I learned to sit with it. Let us see how many you need.
dialogue.niall_post_win_retry: You paid the price and came back and paid it again in a different coin. That is all the game is — all it ever was. What is at Tara will cost you more. You are better able for it now than you were. That is all I can give you.
dialogue.niall_feud_pre: Something between us is unresolved. I know the weight of that — I have carried things unresolved for years and they do not lighten. Sit down. Let the board decide what words cannot.
dialogue.niall_feud_post_win: It is resolved, then — by the pieces, not by me. I do not have to like it. I have respected harder things than this. Go. We are square.
dialogue.niall_feud_post_loss: The board speaks clearly enough. I do not gloat — I have been where you are. What is between us is still between us. Come back when you are ready to finish it.
dialogue.niall_nemesis_pre: Nine losses and I am still here. You and I have become a long argument. I have lost longer arguments than this one. But I have also won them. Sit. It ends today — one way or the other.
dialogue.niall_nemesis_post_win: I have lost to better than Murchadh and I am still breathing. You will remember that when the time comes. It is not the losing that breaks you — it is the not getting up. You have gotten up. That is the whole of it.
